---
title: "Ubuntu 12.10, kernel 3.5.0-25, error installing compat-wireless for wired connection"
date: 2013-03-13
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by himerus on 2013-03-13
hi,
I have Ubuntu 12.10, kernel 3.5.0-25 , and did all the updates. The wired connection is not working and for get it working I must install compat wireless drivers.
I have done this once but I reinstalled Ubuntu and now it doesn't work anymore. 

I did  apt-get install linux-headers-generic build-essential which says everything is up-to-date.
Then ./scripts/driver-select alx which didn't give any error.
Then 'make' which  gave some errors: 

# make
./scripts/gen-compat-autoconf.sh /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/.config /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/config.mk > include/linux/compat_autoconf.h
make -C /lib/modules/3.5.0-25-generic/build M=/root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1 modules
make[1]: Entering directory `/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-25-generic'
  CC [M]  /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/compat/main.o
  CC [M]  /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/compat/compat-3.7.o
/root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/compat/compat-3.7.c: In function ‘pcie_flags_reg’:
/root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/compat/compat-3.7.c:37:2: warning: passing argument 1 of ‘pci_find_capability’ discards ‘const’ qualifier from pointer target type [enabled by default]
In file included from /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/include/linux/compat-3.7.h:10:0,
                 from /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/include/linux/compat-2.6.h:67,
                 from <command-line>:0:
include/linux/pci.h:714:5: note: expected ‘struct pci_dev *’ but argument is of type ‘const struct pci_dev *’
  LD [M]  /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/compat/compat.o
scripts/Makefile.build:44: /root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/drivers/net/ethernet/atheros/alx/Makefile: No such file or directory
make[4]: *** No rule to make target `/root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/drivers/net/ethernet/atheros/alx/Makefile'.  Stop.
make[3]: *** [/root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/drivers/net/ethernet/atheros/alx] Error 2
make[2]: *** [/root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1/drivers/net/ethernet/atheros] Error 2
make[1]: *** [_module_/root/compat-wireless-3.6.8-1] Error 2
make[1]: Leaving directory `/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-25-generic'
make: *** [modules] Error 2

Please help me. I reinstalled Ubuntu very confident that I can remake my wired connection work, but Ubuntu is full of surprises.
Thanks a lot !

---

### Post by chili555 on 2013-03-14
> /lib/modules/3.5.0-25-generic/kernel/ubuntu/alx/alx.koIsn't alx included in 3.5.0-25 by default? Or is it that the included version still doesn't cover your device? Which is your device?```
lspci -nn | grep 0200
```Moreover, the compat-wireless package that covers even more devices is compat-wireless-3.6.8-1-*snpc*. Even if you compiled the package you have, I doubt it covers your device.

---

### Post by himerus on 2013-03-14
hi,
Thanks for replying.

lspci -nn | grep 0200
04:00.0 Ethernet controller [0200]: Atheros Communications Inc. AR8161 Gigabit Ethernet [1969:1091] (rev 08)


I was pretty sure it works because I did this once and it worked. Then I resinstalled Ubuntu, did all the updates, booted with kernel 3.5.0-25,  but the reinstalling of compat wireless same version as previous didn't  worked anymore.

### Post by chili555 on 2013-03-14
> $ modinfo /lib/modules/3.5.0-25-generic/kernel/ubuntu/alx/alx.ko
filename:       /lib/modules/3.5.0-25-generic/kernel/ubuntu/alx/alx.ko
version:        1.2.2
license:        Dual BSD/GPL
description:    Qualcomm Atheros Gigabit Ethernet Driver
author:         Qualcomm Corporation, <nic-devel@qualcomm.com>
srcversion:     064F975FF20C38BFEB63745
alias:          pci:v00001969d000010A0sv*sd*bc*sc*i*
alias:          pci:v00001969d000010A1sv*sd*bc*sc*i*
alias:          pci:v00001969d00001090sv*sd*bc*sc*i*
alias:          pci:v0000[COLOR="#FF0000"]1969[/COLOR]d0000[COLOR="#FF0000"]1091[/COLOR]sv*sd*bc*sc*i*
depends:        mdio
intree:         Y
vermagic:       3.5.0-25-generic SMP mod_unload modversions So why isn't the version already built in to -25 working for you? Any clues here?```
sudo modprobe alx
dmesg | grep -e alx -e eth0
nm-tool
```

### Post by himerus on 2013-03-14
WOOW ... I installed compat-wireless-3.6.8-1-snpc and works perfectly now. 
THANKS A LOT.

---

### Post by chili555 on 2013-03-14
Oooh, nasty! Can you get a temporary wireless connection and do:```
sudo apt-get install linux-backports-modules-cw-3.6-quantal-generic
sudo modprobe -r alx
sudo modprobe alx
```Then check dmesg again for entries after the latest you posted:> [ [COLOR="#FF0000"]9931.969762[/COLOR]] alx 0000:04:00.0: eth0: 17F0: 00000000,00000000,00000000,00000000
```
dmesg | grep -e alx -e eth0
```If the errors persist, we'll report a bug and try compiling again.

---
